question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R3 (P1) removes its SRv6 locator `TRANSIT` (prefix fcbb:bb00:3::/48).
R3 stops being a transit node for SRv6 End/End.X functions. The R3
prefix fcbb:bb00:3::/48 is no longer advertised; the IS-IS LSP
Locator-TLV for TRANSIT disappears. Other PEs/Ps relying on R3 as a
TRANSIT hop in SR policies lose intermediate SIDs. Pure SRv6
underlay → plain IPv6 IS-IS still works for VPNv4 next-hop reachability,
but any SR-TE policy through R3 breaks.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.103 (R3, P1), the SRv6 locator TRANSIT (prefix fcbb:bb00:3::/48) was removed, so R3 stops advertising the locator prefix and its IS-IS Locator-TLV disappears; R3 no longer offers End/End.X transit SIDs.
SR policies relying on R3 as a TRANSIT hop lose their intermediate SIDs, while plain IPv6 IS-IS forwarding (and thus VPNv4 next-hop reachability) still works.
Fix: re-create the SRv6 locator TRANSIT (prefix fcbb:bb00:3::/48) and re-bind it under R3's IS-IS IPv6 AF.
expected_symptoms_summary:
No fault syslog observed after apply — silent scenario; SRv6
locator removal is a quiet config event. The underlay impact is
visible via CLI probe. Diagnostic ground truth lives in
expected_state_diffs.
expected_state_diffs: R3 `show segment-routing srv6 locator`: TRANSIT entry absent (baseline: `TRANSIT  fcbb:bb00:3::/48`).
R3 `show running-config segment-routing srv6 locators`: TRANSIT block removed. Other PEs still advertise their own locators (R2: PE1, R4: PE2).
R2 and R4 `show isis database verbose` (R3's LSPID): no Locator sub-TLV for TRANSIT in R3's IS-IS LSP.
R2 `show route ipv6 fcbb:bb00:3::/48`: Network not in table — the TRANSIT locator prefix is no longer advertised.

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router isis core
 address-family ipv6 unicast
  segment-routing srv6
   no locator TRANSIT
!
segment-routing
 srv6
  locators
   no locator TRANSIT

revert.commands:
configure terminal
segment-routing
 srv6
  locators
   locator TRANSIT
    micro-segment behavior unode psp-usd
    prefix fcbb:bb00:3::/48
!
router isis core
 address-family ipv6 unicast
  segment-routing srv6
   locator TRANSIT
